operating_state: NIKE revenues declined 9% to $11.3 billion in Q3 2025, driven by decreased traffic across NIKE Direct channels and deliberate supply reduction of certain footwear products through increased markdowns. NIKE Brand revenues fell 9% with footwear declining 12% and apparel down 3%, while wholesale revenues decreased 7% and NIKE Direct revenues dropped 12%. Performance weakened globally with double-digit declines across Greater China, EMEA, and APLA regions, while North America declined modestly. Operating cash flow deteriorated with $648 million outflow for the first nine months.
cost_structure: Gross margin contracted 330 basis points to 41.5% primarily due to lower average selling prices from higher discounts, elevated inventory obsolescence reserves, and higher product costs. Operating overhead expense decreased 13% due to prior year restructuring charges and lower wage-related expenses, while demand creation expense increased 8% reflecting investment in brand marketing. Total selling and administrative expense decreased 8% but increased as percentage of revenues to 34.5%.
material_events: The company returned approximately $1.1 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases during the third quarter, with total share repurchases of $2.8 billion at an average price of $80.02 per share. Management implemented strategic actions to reduce supply of certain footwear products while repositioning NIKE Brand Digital as a full-price platform and reinvesting in wholesale distribution. Nike entered into new credit facilities totaling $3 billion and repaid $1 billion in maturing notes.
macro_exposures: The company faces uncertainty from geopolitical dynamics, new tariffs, tax regulation changes, and fluctuating foreign exchange rates that could materially impact consumer behavior and profitability. Foreign currency translation negatively affected revenues by approximately $310 million and income before taxes by $67 million for the quarter.
forward_outlook: Management expects the strategic actions to reduce supply and rebalance product portfolio will continue negatively impacting revenues and gross margin over the next several quarters, along with higher demand creation expenses. The company believes these actions will reignite brand momentum and reposition the business for long-term shareholder value creation.
uncertainty_profile: Inventory management remains challenging as the company balances supply reduction with new product introductions while managing elevated obsolescence reserves. The effectiveness and timeline of strategic repositioning efforts to restore brand momentum represents execution risk for future performance recovery. Endorsement contract obligations increased to $15.1 billion with $1.4 billion payable within 12 months.
